title: Goldman Sachs Warns on AI’s Debt Tsunami — Is This the End of the AI Boom?
excerpt: Goldman Sachs is sounding alarms as hyperscalers pile on debt at a pace the credit markets are struggling to absorb, and the window for AI companies to prove...
content_html:
Rich Duprey

Sun, July 19, 2026 at 10:07 AM CDT 4 min read

Quick Read

  • The six largest AI spenders issued $244 billion in bonds this year, 14 times 2024 levels, as the AI arms race rapidly reshapes balance sheets.

  • Goldman Sachs reports hyperscaler leverage ratios doubled to 1.8x in six months, with $5.8 trillion in projected AI capex through 2030.

  • Credit default swap spreads widened far beyond the broader market, signaling investors doubt AI spending can generate returns fast enough.

  • Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

Artificial intelligence has become the defining investment theme of the decade, with hyperscalers committing hundreds of billions to data centers, chips, power, and software. The spending has rewarded companies across the AI supply chain, but markets are shifting from excitement to accountability.

Investors now want proof that massive capital expenditures can produce lasting returns. Banks and Goldman Sachs traders are warning that AI infrastructure spending is increasingly fueled by debt, while credit markets show growing concern as borrowing accelerates faster than near-term cash generation.

AI Spending Has Become a Debt-Fueled Race

The AI arms race is rapidly reshaping balance sheets. Companies are issuing bonds at unprecedented scale to fund data centers, expand energy capacity, and secure scarce computing resources. The six largest AI infrastructure spenders -- Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG), Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META), Oracle (NYSE:ORCL), Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), and SpaceX (NASDAQ:SPCX) -- have issued a combined $244 billion in bonds this year, more than double last year's total and 14 times 2024 levels.

This surge in borrowing reflects the enormous capital requirements behind AI. Building the infrastructure needed to support advanced models requires billions of dollars before those investments begin producing meaningful revenue. Unlike traditional software businesses, where additional users can often be added at minimal cost, AI requires expensive physical infrastructure.

Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

Goldman Sachs reports that hyperscaler leverage ratios have doubled from 0.9x to 1.8x in roughly six months. Market indigestion is evident: Goldman's AI bond basket spreads widened sharply, and the supply pain threshold has collapsed -- where $75 billion once stressed the market, just $25 billion now puts it on the defensive.

content_html_zh:
里奇·杜普雷

2026 年 7 月 19 日星期日上午 10:07 CDT 阅读时间 4 分钟

快速阅读

  • 随着人工智能军备竞赛迅速重塑资产负债表，六大人工智能支出国今年发行了 2440 亿美元的债券，是 2024 年水平的 14 倍。
  • 高盛报告称，超大规模企业的杠杆率在六个月内翻了一番，达到 1.8 倍，预计到 2030 年人工智能资本支出将达到 5.8 万亿美元。
  • 信用违约掉期利差远远超出了大盘，这表明投资者怀疑人工智能支出能否足够快地产生回报。
  • 不要等待：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚透露了他最喜欢的 10 只人工智能股票。 立即免费查看完整列表。

人工智能已成为这十年来的决定性投资主题，超大规模企业在数据中心、芯片、电力和软件方面投入了数千亿美元。 这些支出给整个人工智能供应链上的公司带来了回报，但市场正在从兴奋转向负责任。

投资者现在希望有证据证明大规模的资本支出可以产生持久的回报。 银行和高盛交易员警告称，人工智能基础设施支出越来越多地受到债务的推动，而信贷市场则表现出越来越多的担忧，因为借贷增速快于近期现金生成速度。

人工智能支出已成为一场债务驱动的竞赛

人工智能军备竞赛正在迅速重塑资产负债表。 公司正在以前所未有的规模发行债券，为数据中心提供资金、扩大能源容量并确保稀缺的计算资源。 六大人工智能基础设施支出企业——微软（纳斯达克股票代码：MSFT）、亚马逊（纳斯达克股票代码：AMZN）、Alphabet（纳斯达克股票代码：GOOG）、Meta Platforms（纳斯达克股票代码：META）、甲骨文（纽约证券交易所代码：ORCL）、英伟达（纳斯达克股票代码：NVDA）和SpaceX（纳斯达克股票代码：SPCX）——今年已发行了总计2440亿美元的债券，是去年总额的两倍多，是2024年水平的14倍。

借贷的激增反映了人工智能背后巨大的资本需求。 建设支持先进模型所需的基础设施需要数十亿美元，然后这些投资才能开始产生有意义的收入。 与通常可以以最低成本添加额外用户的传统软件企业不同，人工智能需要昂贵的物理基础设施。

不要等待：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚透露了他最喜欢的 10 只人工智能股票。 立即免费查看完整列表。

高盛报告称，超大型企业的杠杆率在大约六个月内从 0.9 倍翻了一番至 1.8 倍。 市场消化不良是显而易见的：高盛的人工智能债券一揽子利差急剧扩大，供应痛苦阈值已经崩溃——曾经有750亿美元给市场带来压力，现在只有250亿美元让市场处于守势。
